stem: According to the 2026 RISC guidance, which statement about regulatory exposure limits to radioactive materials is correct?

options.text: Workers likely to receive more than 100 mrem (1 mSv)/year must receive adequate training to protect themselves against radiation.
Workers likely to receive more than 100 mrem (1 mSv)/year need not receive adequate training to protect themselves against radiation.
Workers likely to receive less than 100 mrem (1 mSv)/year must receive adequate training to protect themselves against radiation.
Workers likely to receive more than 200 mrem (1 mSv)/year must receive adequate training to protect themselves against radiation.

explanation: Correct answer: A. Workers likely to receive more than 100 mrem (1 mSv)/year must receive adequate training to protect themselves against radiation. This point is drawn from Chapter 4, section '4.1 Regulatory exposure limits to radioactive materials', and is tested because it is a practical safety rule, regulatory requirement, high-yield distinction, or commonly confused threshold.
